target_audience: Affluent families aged 38-60 in the Austin metro with household income $350k+ planning a custom home build on land they already own or are about to buy

pain_points_solutions.pain_point: Custom builds that blow 40% over budget and finish a year late
pain_points_solutions.solution: Fixed-price contracts with a written on-time guarantee backed by liquidated damages if we miss the date

pain_points_solutions.pain_point: Builders who disappear after framing and subcontract out quality control to whoever's cheapest
pain_points_solutions.solution: Dedicated project manager on-site daily plus weekly owner walkthroughs with photo reports

pain_points_solutions.pain_point: Finding out six months in that your builder can't actually build the architect's plans
pain_points_solutions.solution: In-house architect and engineer review before contract signing so surprises don't happen mid-build

offer: Free custom build feasibility report including budget estimate, timeline, and lot analysis — normally $2,500
risk_reversal: If we go over our written budget by even one dollar, we eat the difference
guarantees: 10-year structural warranty, 2-year workmanship warranty, fixed-price contracts
limited_availability: Only taking 6 new custom home projects for 2026 — 2 slots remaining
lead_magnet: The Austin Custom Build Cost Guide 2026: real numbers from 40 recent builds
top_stats: Built 120+ custom homes across Travis and Hays County since 2011
98% of projects delivered on or before contracted completion date
Average project size: 4,800 sqft at $385 per square foot
service_area: Austin, Westlake, Lakeway, Dripping Springs, and the Texas Hill Country
